explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.findings: The company's 2023 10-K filing highlights a commitment to complying with evolving AI regulations and laws. An article also references aligning with regulatory compliance and establishing operational processes for accountability related to AI hiring tools, underscoring policy requirements for external accountability.
external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Publish third-party audit results, certifications, or regulatory compliance documentation.

relevant_sources.summary: This 2023 10-K filing from Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. provides evidence for **governance**, **privacy**, **external accountability**, and **fairness**. The filing discusses the company's assessment of AI risks, including regulatory, data privacy, and cybersecurity concerns, indicating a governance framework for managing these issues. It also highlights the company's commitment to complying with evolving AI regulations and laws, demonstrating external accountability, and acknowledges potential biases and ethical considerations related to AI, supporting the fairness pillar.
relevant_sources.title: Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023

relevant_sources.summary: This article from Woodruff Sawyer supports the **external_accountability**, **fairness**, **governance**, and **oversight** pillars of responsible AI. It highlights the necessity of human monitoring and regular bias audits for AI hiring tools to prevent discrimination and disparate impact, aligning with regulatory compliance and establishing operational processes for accountability. The article emphasizes the need for evaluating AI for bias and auditing for disparate impact, underscoring policy requirements for fairness and external accountability.
relevant_sources.title: Human monitoring of AI necessary to oversee hiring processes, limit discrimination claims

transparency.path_to_improvement: Document AI systems in use, including vendor/third-party AI tools.

summary.overall_findings: Arthur J. Gallagher & Co.'s published materials address fairness and oversight through specific practices, such as acknowledging potential biases in AI and highlighting the necessity of human monitoring for AI hiring tools. These are among 5 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars for which documented evidence was found, with privacy, governance, and external accountability also addressed at the policy level. For instance, Arthur J. Gallagher & Co.'s 2023 10-K filing discusses the assessment of AI risks, including data privacy concerns, and indicates a governance framework for managing these risks. No qualifying public evidence was found for transparency or explainability, based on a review of 9 publicly available sources.
